reviews.review_description: Excellent image quality and architecture quality. But not the best narrative.

developer_response.body: Thanks for checking back in. We take your comments on board so hopefully we'll get everything just right for Parti 3.
Thanks for checking back in! And glad you enjoyed it.

reviews.review_description: This home was breathtaking... and a tease at that.. I visualized living there and stayed inside that app as long as I could.  This is a house I could only dream about.  Please send us more.  Great job guys 👍
It tells me to update my oculus app but my  oculus app is already up to date. I can't give 5 stars to something that isn't 100% functional

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you are having problems. If you could email us your device version and the Oculus app version you are running we will look into the problem. Email: dboxphones@gmail.com

reviews.review_description: Parti really places you in exclusive modern architectural buildings. You can really experience the space of these impressive homes in full detail and get an introduction from the architect. Can't wait for the next one!!

developer_response.body: Thanks! Glad you like it. Check back soon and we'll have another experience ready for you to explore.

reviews.review_description: The two offerings from Parti are really wonderful. My partner is an architecture student and we have spent such a long time marvelling over these incredible walk-throughs. It’s impossible to sit down and do the whole thing from beginning to end if someone is near you, because you keep on wanting to take off the headset and share it. The homes are breathtaking. It’s a wonderful dream for anyone who would like to dream of living in a home designed by world-class architect. Brilliant for anybody who is interested in design, architecture, shape, form, and rationale. Our only regret is at the moment there are only two buildings to experience. We would love it if they were walk-throughs of the major architectural spaces like Farnsworth House etc etc.
